_meta.description: Curated slang reference for youth mental health chatbot training data generation. Injected as a sample reference block in generation prompts to improve register authenticity. Organized by language × age_group × domain.
_meta.usage: Sample 3-5 terms per call matching the persona's language + age_group. Include at least 1 term from mental_health_euphemisms when risk=high.
_meta.note_on_mix: Mix entries reflect Canadian bilingual youth code-switching (Quebec/Ontario contexts). Phrases blend FR and EN mid-sentence — not alternating languages but genuine hybridization.
_meta.note_on_mental_health: Mental health euphemisms exist primarily to evade algorithmic moderation. They are used both genuinely (by users in distress) and hyperbolically (by users joking). The classifier must handle both registers.

provenance.primary_source: Claude training knowledge (cutoff May 2025). The majority of entries — especially EN Gen Z slang, FR Quebec register, mix code-switching patterns, and mental health algospeak — come from training data, not live web sources.
web_research_confirmed: menty b, grippy sock vacation, bed rot, brain rot — confirmed via search snippet (gabb.com / themindsjournal.com)
ana, mia, thinspo, bonespo, TW/CW — confirmed via Frontiers/ResearchGate search results on pro-ED communities
Quebec youth influenced by French YouTubers: wesh, du coup, daron — confirmed via Radio-Canada search snippet
Gen Z terms rizz, slay, no cap, delulu, it's giving — confirmed via search snippets (European Youth Portal, OpenL)
provenance.validation_needed: This bank has NOT been reviewed by a youth mental health professional, a Quebec French native youth speaker, or a community member with lived experience. Terms should be validated before deployment, particularly: (1) mental health euphemisms for accuracy and currency, (2) FR Quebec entries for authenticity, (3) mix/code-switching patterns for regional accuracy.
provenance.recommended_validation: Share with KHP team members or youth advisors familiar with Quebec French youth register and online safety language before using in generation.

general_youth: i don't wanna talk about it
social_media: they left me out of the groupchat

mental_health_euphemisms: kms / kys (kill myself/yourself — used both hyperbolically and seriously)
grippy sock vacation (psychiatric hospitalization, said lightly)
bed rot (lying in bed, can signal depression)
brain rot (from too much screen time, but also used for dissociation)
ana / mia (anorexia / bulimia, pro-ED community terms)
thinspo / bonespo (thinspiration — used in ED communities)
vent (to vent about distress, sometimes a signal to engage seriously)
tw / cw (trigger warning / content warning, placed before describing distress)
I'm not okay but I'm fine
social_media: it blew up (went viral, positive or embarrassing)

general_youth: I just don't see the point
mental_health_euphemisms: be so fr — I don't wanna be here
I'm exhausted in a different way
I'm not okay and it's not a joke this time
passive ideation (used casually by mentally-literate youth)
OD'd (overdosed, can be framed as accidental)
I'm in a really bad place
I keep thinking about ways out
social_media: algorithm is feeding me bad stuff
they're posting for engagement not because they care

general_youth: I've kind of given up on
I've been better, I've been worse
mental_health_euphemisms: passive suicidal ideation (clinical term used casually)
I've been having dark thoughts again
I'm not in a great headspace
I don't see myself in the future
I don't know why I'm still here
I'm really struggling to see the point
I checked out of my own life

general_youth: j'en peux pu (QC — j'en peux plus)
c'est pas correct (QC — c'est pas bien)
mental_health_euphemisms: en finir (vouloir mettre fin à ses jours)
me suicider (direct, parfois dit à la légère)
je suis à bout (QC — au bout du rouleau)
j'en peux pu pantoute (QC — totalement épuisé/désespéré)
je veux juste dormir pour toujours
je me sens comme un fardeau
se blesser / se faire du mal (SH — self-harm)
ana / mia (troubles alimentaires, termes communauté pro-ED)
vent (partager sa détresse — emprunté de l'anglais)
tw (trigger warning — utilisé même en français)
je veux unalive (algospeak utilisé même en FR)
je veux kms (kms utilisé tel quel en FR)
social_media: ça a buzzé (ça a été viral)
ils ont posté un truc sur moi
ils ont fait un groupchat sans moi

general_youth: c'est correct (QC — c'est ok, sous-entendu: non c'est pas correct)
dans ma tête c'est le chaos
mental_health_euphemisms: je vois pas pourquoi je continuerais
idéations passives (terme clinique utilisé par les jeunes informés)
je me sens comme un boulet (fardeau)
les autres seraient mieux sans moi
grippy sock vacation (utilisé même en FR par des jeunes bilingues)
social_media: l'algorithme me nourrit de trucs toxiques
no-contact era (même terme utilisé en FR)

mental_health_euphemisms: j'ai pas un bon rapport à la bouffe
je me sens comme un fardeau
les autres feraient mieux sans moi
j'ai du mal à me projeter
j'ai fait une tentative (dit parfois après les faits)

mix._note: Mix entries model Canadian bilingual youth code-switching, primarily Quebec French + English. These are full hybrid phrases, not alternating languages. Most prevalent in ages 13-22.

general_youth: je suis full tired right now
mon prof est literally the worst
mental_health_euphemisms: j'ai eu un full menty b
j'ai des intrusive thoughts full intenses

general_youth: c'est vraiment a lot en ce moment
j'suis in a really bad place là
j'ai kind of given up on
j'suis done for real cette fois
je vois pas the point anymore

j'ai pensé à en finir for real
j'me sens comme I don't belong anywhere
